notes: Evolved into IBM Tivoli Identity Manager (2001) then IBM Security Identity Manager; TME 10 brand retired ~2002
Base management framework; TME brand retired 2002 when Tivoli became IBM Tivoli Software
Still active on IBM mainframes (z/OS); long-running mainframe security standard
Still available via Broadcom (acquired CA Technologies 2018); legacy mainframe security
RACF-style security for Unix; absorbed into IBM Tivoli products; no longer separately marketed
OSF DCE; essentially obsolete by 2000s; superseded by Kerberos/LDAP/Active Directory
Still active as core authentication in Active Directory and many enterprise systems
Succeeded by Windows 2000 Server and Windows Server 2003+
Novell NetWare market share collapsed after 2000; product EOL 2009; replaced by Active Directory
Superseded by z/OS in 2001; IBM mainframe OS lineage continues
